observations: Overall layout structure matches Figma: charcoal rounded panel containing heading area, progress bar with navigation, and card carousel
Cards are arranged in 3-column grid at 1440px matching Figma layout
Charcoal panel has correct rounded corners (~24px / rounded-3xl) matching Figma 25px
Content is not clipped at any edge of the charcoal panel
Navigation arrows and progress bar are positioned correctly relative to the carousel content
Gap between stacked carousel sections is visible and appropriate -- each section's charcoal panel is clearly separated by the light page background

focusedChecks.implementationValue: ~32px visible padding inside charcoal (pseudo-element extends -32px beyond container via before:-inset-8 at md+)
observations: The heading text sits approximately 30-40px below the top edge of the charcoal panel
The bottom of the cards/links sits approximately 30-40px above the bottom edge of the charcoal panel
This is less generous than the Figma design but the content is NOT cramped -- there is visible breathing room

The previous implementation had py-16 md:py-24 (96px) on the container PLUS py-10 sm:py-16 (64px) on the section, creating 160px of combined vertical padding which was visibly excessive (double padding)
The fix correctly removed the container-level padding to eliminate the double-padding problem
focusedChecks.recommendation: The current spacing is a reasonable fix for the double-padding issue. The charcoal panel's inner padding (32px) is tighter than Figma (96px) but visually acceptable. If a closer Figma match is desired, the pseudo-element vertical inset could be increased to before:-inset-y-16 (64px) at md+ while keeping horizontal at before:-inset-x-8 (32px). However, this would need care to ensure the charcoal doesn't extend beyond the section's padding zone (currently 64px at sm+).

observations: The double padding issue is resolved -- there is no visible excessive spacing around the charcoal panels
Section py-10 sm:py-16 (64px) provides consistent spacing between sections
The charcoal pseudo-element extends beyond the container but stays within the section padding zone (64px section padding > 32px pseudo-element overshoot)

focusedChecks.check: Content clipping at charcoal panel edges
observations: No content is clipped at any edge of the charcoal panel
Cards, headings, progress bar, navigation arrows are all fully visible within the charcoal area
The last visible card in each row is not cut off horizontally
The gap between consecutive carousel sections is visible in the full-page screenshot
A light/cream page background is visible between each charcoal panel
The gap appears to be approximately 64px (2 x section py-16 = 2 x 32px from each section's border to charcoal) which creates clear visual separation
Sections do not overlap or appear jammed together
The charcoal background fully covers the content area without visible gaps
The rounded corners (rounded-3xl / ~24px) are consistent and match the Figma 25px radius
No light-background bleed-through is visible between the charcoal edge and content
The negative inset approach successfully creates a full-bleed charcoal panel within the container structure

overallAssessment.summary: The double-padding fix successfully eliminates the excessive spacing. The charcoal panel's inner vertical padding (approximately 32px) is tighter than the Figma design (96px), which is a noticeable but not critical difference. Content is properly contained, not clipped, and the gap between stacked sections is visually appropriate. The fix achieves its goal of removing double padding without breaking the layout.
passedChecks: Content not clipped at charcoal edges
Gap between stacked sections is appropriate
Overall composition and element positioning matches Figma
overallAssessment.optionalEnhancement: To more closely match Figma's 96px inner padding, consider splitting the pseudo-element inset into separate x/y values: before:-inset-x-(--side-gutter) md:before:-inset-x-8 before:-inset-y-16 md:before:-inset-y-24. This would give ~96px vertical padding inside the charcoal at md+ while maintaining the current 32px horizontal extension. The section padding (py-10 sm:py-16 = 64px) would need to increase to py-16 sm:py-24 (96px) to prevent the charcoal from extending beyond the section boundary.
